title: Tangy Brine-Wet Vietnamese White Navy Beans Bowl
description: A tangy, velvety, and energizing Vietnamese-inspired dish featuring dried white navy beans, brine-wet, and gluten-free-friendly, served in a molcajete.
instructions: Rinse the dried white navy beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours.
Drain and rinse the soaked white navy beans, then add them to a large pot with the ferric chloride brine and 2 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
In a separate pot, combine the coconut water, rice vinegar, sesame oil, chili flakes, salt, and habanero pepper.
Heat the coconut water mixture over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the fresh cilantro and lime juice to the coconut water mixture and stir to combine.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the cooked white navy beans in a molcajete, then spoon the coconut water mixture over the top.
Garnish with additional cilantro and serve immediately.
